text: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced a new partnership with Google at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland on January 23, 2025.
Dr. Sarah Chen from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology published a groundbreaking study on climate change impacts in the journal Nature.
The European Central Bank, headquartered in Frankfurt, raised interest rates by 0.25% following a meeting chaired by President Christine Lagarde.
SpaceX successfully launched its Starship rocket from Boca Chica, Texas on March 14, carrying supplies destined for the International Space Station.
Nobel laureate Malala Yousafzai spoke at the United Nations General Assembly in New York about expanding girls' education in Pakistan and Afghanistan.
Amazon Web Services opened three new data centers in Mumbai, India as part of a $12.7 billion infrastructure investment announced by CEO Andy Jassy.
The World Health Organization declared the Marburg virus outbreak in Tanzania contained after six weeks of coordinated response led by Dr. Tedros Ghebreyesus.
Microsoft Research published a paper at the Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems demonstrating that their new language model outperformed GPT-4 on mathematical reasoning.
French President Emmanuel Macron met with Chinese Premier Li Qiang in Beijing to discuss trade agreements and the ongoing conflict in Ukraine.
The Federal Reserve Bank of New York reported that household debt in the United States reached $17.5 trillion in the fourth quarter of 2024.
Researchers at Stanford University and the Max Planck Institute for Astrophysics discovered a new exoplanet orbiting Proxima Centauri, just 4.24 light-years from Earth.
Toyota Motor Corporation announced it would invest $8 billion in a new battery factory in Greensboro, North Carolina, creating approximately 5,000 jobs by 2027.
The Olympic Committee confirmed that Brisbane, Australia will host the 2032 Summer Olympics, with events spread across Queensland.
Professor James Rodriguez of Columbia University won the Pulitzer Prize for his investigation into pollution along the Mississippi River published in The Washington Post.
Netflix reported 260 million subscribers worldwide after launching its ad-supported tier across 12 new markets in Southeast Asia and Latin America.
